questions: How do I create an account?
Can I register as a receiver?
answer: You can create a receiver account from the Register page. After registering, log in and complete your profile before booking a service.

questions: What roles does Homefix support?
answer: Homefix supports three roles: receivers book services, providers complete assigned appointments, and managers oversee platform operations.

questions: Can I switch my role?
answer: Roles are controlled by the platform account setup. If you need a different role, contact a manager instead of creating duplicate accounts.

questions: Why can't I access a page?
answer: Some pages are role-specific. Receivers see booking and customer tools, providers see assigned work and skills, and managers see platform administration pages.

questions: How do I log out?
answer: Use the logout button in the top bar. This clears your local session token and returns you to the login flow.

questions: How do I update my profile?
answer: Go to My Profile to update profile details such as display name, phone number, and role-specific information available to your account.

questions: Can I change my email?
answer: Email changes may be managed by account administration. If the profile page does not allow the change, contact a manager.

questions: Why should my phone number be accurate?
answer: Providers and managers may use your phone number to coordinate service details, so keep it accurate in your profile.

questions: What profile data does a provider see?
answer: Providers can see appointment-related information needed to complete the job, such as receiver name and service address. They do not receive password or authentication data.

questions: Can I keep using Homefix if my account is inactive?
answer: Inactive accounts may be blocked from normal use. Contact a manager if you believe your account status is wrong.

questions: How do I add an address?
answer: Go to My Profile and add a service address. You need at least one address before creating an appointment.

questions: What is a default address?
answer: A default address is your primary service location. Homefix shows it first when you book appointments.

questions: Can I have multiple addresses?
answer: Yes. You can save multiple addresses and choose the right one during appointment creation.

questions: Can a provider see all my addresses?
answer: Providers only need the address connected to their assigned appointment. They should not receive unrelated saved addresses.

questions: Can I delete an address?
answer: You can remove addresses from your profile unless existing appointment records still depend on them. Historical appointments keep their linked address for record integrity.

questions: How do I book a service?
How can I create an appointment?
answer: Go to Book Service or My Appointments, choose your address, select a service and qualified provider, pick a time, confirm the time, and submit the appointment.

questions: Why do I need to select both service and provider?
answer: Homefix only allows appointments with provider-service pairs that are approved by a manager. Selecting one filters the other so the booking stays valid.

questions: Why is a provider unavailable?
answer: A provider may be unavailable because they already have a pending, accepted, or in-progress appointment, or because they added an unavailable time block to their schedule.

questions: Can I book outside business hours?
answer: Yes. Normal provider hours are Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Weekday after-hours bookings add 20%, weekend bookings add 10%, and weekend after-hours bookings add 30%.

questions: Why do I have to confirm the scheduled time?
answer: The confirm step helps prevent accidental bookings. Choose or enter a time, then click Confirm before creating the appointment.

questions: What does pending mean?
answer: Pending means the appointment was created and is waiting for the assigned provider to accept or reject it.

questions: What does accepted mean?
answer: Accepted means the provider has agreed to perform the service. The provider can then move it to in progress when work starts.

questions: What does in progress mean?
answer: In progress means the provider has started the service workflow. After the work is done, the receiver confirms completion.

questions: What does completed mean?
answer: Completed means the receiver confirmed the service was finished. Payments and reviews become available after completion and actual hours are set.

questions: What does no show mean?
answer: No show indicates the appointment could not be completed because one party did not appear or the service could not proceed as scheduled.

questions: How do I cancel an appointment?
answer: Receivers can cancel appointments while they are pending or accepted. Go to My Appointments and change the status to cancelled if the option is available.

questions: Can I cancel a completed appointment?
answer: Completed appointments cannot normally be cancelled because they are part of payment and review history. Contact a manager if there is an issue.

questions: Can a provider reject my appointment?
answer: Yes. Providers can reject pending appointments. If that happens, you can create a new appointment with another qualified provider.

questions: What happens to payment after cancellation?
answer: Payments are only created after completion and actual hours. A cancelled appointment should not require a new payment.

questions: Can I rebook after cancelling?
answer: Yes. You can create a new appointment and choose a new provider, service, time, and address.

questions: How do I choose a provider?
answer: Choose a service first to see qualified providers, or choose a provider first to see the services they are approved to offer.

questions: Why is a provider missing from booking?
answer: A provider only appears for a service if their skill has been approved by a manager and their account is active.

questions: Can I see provider ratings?
answer: Provider ratings come from receiver reviews after completed appointments. You can ask AI about public provider ratings or view reviews where available.

questions: Can a provider create appointments for me?
answer: No. Providers cannot create appointments. Receivers and managers create appointments; providers respond to assigned requests.

questions: What does provider unavailable time mean?
answer: Providers can add unavailable time blocks. Homefix prevents bookings that overlap those blocks or other active appointments.

questions: What services are available?
answer: Homefix seed data includes Plumbing, Electrical, Cleaning, Painting, and Landscaping. Managers can maintain the service catalog.

questions: Can every provider do every service?
answer: No. Providers must have an approved skill link for each service before they can be booked for it.

questions: How are service prices set?
answer: Each approved provider-service link has a base hourly rate. Booking time surcharges are stored separately and applied when the appointment total is calculated.

questions: Can a service be deleted?
answer: Managers can delete services only when database relationships allow it. Services referenced by appointments may be protected by foreign keys.

questions: How can I find cleaning providers?
answer: Ask which providers offer cleaning or select Cleaning when creating an appointment. Homefix will list approved active providers for that service.

questions: How is my appointment total calculated?
answer: The estimate is base hourly rate at booking multiplied by (1 + schedule surcharge rate) and estimated hours, plus tip. The final total uses actual hours after the provider records them.

questions: What is hourly rate snapshot?
answer: The appointment saves the provider base hourly rate and the schedule surcharge rate separately, so later provider price changes do not affect that appointment.

questions: When is there a 20 percent surcharge?
answer: A 20% surcharge applies for weekday bookings outside Monday-Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM normal provider hours.

questions: When is there a weekend surcharge?
answer: A 10% surcharge applies to weekend bookings. If the weekend booking is also after-hours, the surcharge becomes 30% total.

questions: Why did my final total change?
answer: Final total uses actual service hours recorded after provider acceptance. It may differ from the estimate if the actual work took more or less time.

questions: How do I pay?
answer: After an appointment is completed and actual hours are set, go to My Payments and create a payment. Receiver payments are paid from wallet balance.

questions: How do I recharge my wallet?
answer: Go to My Payments and use the wallet recharge panel to add funds to your receiver wallet.

questions: Can I pay before completion?
answer: No. Payments can only be created after the appointment is completed and actual hours are available.

questions: What if my wallet balance is too low?
answer: Recharge your wallet first. The payment will not be marked paid if the wallet does not have enough balance.

questions: What is provider payout?
answer: Provider payout is the payment total minus the platform commission. Managers can see commission and payout details in Payments.

questions: Can I get a refund?
answer: Refunds are handled by managers. If a paid payment is changed to refunded, the receiver wallet is credited back.

questions: Can AI issue a refund?
answer: No. AI can explain refund rules, but only managers can update payment status in the system.

questions: What happens when payment is refunded?
answer: A refunded paid payment returns funds to the receiver wallet and keeps the historical payment record.

questions: Can unpaid payments be refunded?
answer: Unpaid payments have not deducted wallet funds, so they do not need a wallet refund.

questions: Who should I contact about billing problems?
answer: Contact a manager or platform support if a payment amount, refund, or wallet balance looks wrong.

questions: When can I write a review?
answer: You can review the provider after your appointment is completed. The Rate button appears for completed appointments.

questions: Can the provider review me?
answer: Yes. Providers can review receivers after completed appointments using the provider-to-receiver review direction.

questions: Can I review twice?
answer: Each appointment allows one receiver-to-provider review and one provider-to-receiver review. Duplicate reviews in the same direction are blocked.

questions: Can a manager write reviews?
answer: No. Managers can view and moderate data, but they cannot create receiver or provider reviews.

questions: Where can I see my reviews?
answer: Go to My Reviews to see reviews connected to your completed appointments.

questions: How are ratings calculated?
answer: Provider ratings are based on receiver-to-provider reviews after completed appointments. Average ratings may be shown in provider analytics.

questions: What rating values are allowed?
answer: Ratings must be whole numbers from 1 to 5. Higher numbers indicate better service experience.

questions: Can I change a rating?
answer: The current app supports creating and deleting reviews based on role permissions. Contact a manager if a review needs correction.

questions: Do low ratings affect providers?
answer: Managers can review low-rating providers and operational risks through reports and Manager AI Assistant.

questions: Can AI invent provider ratings?
answer: No. AI should only answer rating questions using existing reviews and safe provider data from Homefix.

questions: Can AI see other receivers data?
answer: No. Receiver AI can only use your own appointments, addresses, payments, and reviews, plus public provider and service data.

questions: Can AI see passwords?
answer: No. Password hashes, API keys, and authentication secrets are never returned to AI chat responses.

questions: What data is sent to AI providers?
answer: Only safe backend-built context is sent to the AI provider. The frontend never sends API keys and the AI never receives direct database access.

questions: Can I ask for another customer's appointment?
answer: No. Receiver AI will refuse requests for another receiver's private appointments, payments, addresses, or reviews.

questions: Does Homefix expose my wallet details to providers?
answer: Providers do not need your wallet balance. Payment and wallet data are scoped to receivers and managers.

questions: What should I do if I feel unsafe?
answer: If there is an urgent safety issue, stop the service interaction and contact local emergency services. Then notify a manager or support.

questions: Are providers approved for services?
answer: Providers must have manager-approved service skills before they can be booked for that service.

questions: Can a provider be suspended?
answer: Managers can set provider status such as active, resting, inactive, or suspended. Suspended providers should not be used for new bookings.

questions: Can AI perform unsafe actions?
answer: No. AI can answer questions from safe context, but it cannot execute SQL, change data, issue refunds, or bypass permissions.

questions: How does Homefix avoid double booking?
answer: Appointment creation checks provider active appointments and provider unavailable blocks so the same provider cannot serve two appointments at the same time.

questions: Why is the page not loading?
answer: Try refreshing the page and checking that Docker services are running. If the issue continues, ask a manager or developer to check the API health endpoint.

questions: Why can't I create an appointment?
answer: Common causes include no saved address, missing service/provider selection, unconfirmed time, provider unavailable time, or provider-service not approved.

questions: Why can't I pay?
answer: Payment requires a completed appointment, actual hours, and enough wallet balance if the payment is marked paid.

questions: Why can't I review?
answer: Reviews are only available for completed appointments. Each direction can only be reviewed once.

questions: Why is a provider not listed for a service?
answer: The provider may be inactive, missing an approved skill for that service, or filtered out by service/provider selection rules.

questions: Does Homefix send notifications?
answer: The current app focuses on dashboard and page-based status tracking. Check My Appointments and My Payments for current status.

questions: How do I know a provider accepted?
answer: Check My Appointments. The appointment status changes from pending to accepted when the provider accepts it.

questions: How do I know payment is due?
answer: Check My Payments after completion. Unpaid payments or completed appointments without payment indicate payment action may be needed.

questions: Will I be told if a provider rejects?
answer: Check My Appointments. Rejected appointments are shown with the rejected status so you can book another provider.

questions: Where do I track updates?
answer: Use the Dashboard and My Appointments pages to track appointment status, payment status, and review activity.

questions: Who can help me with an account problem?
answer: Contact a manager or platform support for account access, role, or profile issues that you cannot solve from My Profile.

questions: Who can help with billing?
answer: Managers can review payments, refunds, wallet balance issues, commissions, and provider payouts.

questions: Who handles provider issues?
answer: Managers oversee provider status, approved skills, and platform operations. Report provider issues to a manager.

questions: Can AI contact support for me?
answer: AI can guide you to the right page and explain rules, but it does not contact support or modify records for you.

questions: What should I include when reporting a problem?
answer: Include appointment ID, service, provider name, payment ID if relevant, and a concise description of what went wrong.

questions: What can AI Support help with?
answer: AI Support can answer FAQ questions, summarize your own appointments, addresses, payments, reviews, and explain public provider/service information.

questions: What can AI not do?
answer: AI cannot change database records, create payments, issue refunds, reveal secrets, or access another receiver's private data.

questions: Does AI execute SQL?
answer: No. AI never executes SQL. The backend uses predefined safe query functions and sends only safe context to the AI provider.

questions: Why does AI say it does not know?
answer: AI should only answer from FAQ and safe backend data. If that context does not contain the answer, it will say it does not have enough information.

questions: Can AI help me choose a provider?
answer: AI can summarize public provider/service data such as active providers, approved services, rates, and ratings, but you still choose the appointment details.

questions: What is Homefix?
answer: Homefix is a home service booking platform connecting receivers with providers for services such as plumbing, electrical work, cleaning, painting, and landscaping.

questions: What is the main workflow?
answer: Receivers book appointments, providers accept and perform work, receivers confirm completion, payments use actual hours, and both sides can review after completion.

questions: What is the dashboard for?
answer: The dashboard summarizes role-specific activity such as appointments, payments, reviews, saved addresses, provider performance, or manager operations.

questions: Can I use Homefix without Docker?
answer: Developers can run services locally, but the recommended project setup uses Docker Compose from the code folder.

questions: Where should I start?
answer: If you are a receiver, start with My Profile to add an address, then Book Service to create an appointment.
